ogTitle: Watsons Bay vs Wattamolla: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Watsons Bay and Wattamolla.
heading: Watsons Bay vs Wattamolla — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Watsons Bay, New South Wales and Wattamolla, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Watsons Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Wattamolla (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Watsons Bay or Wattamolla safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Watsons Bay has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Wattamolla scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Watsons Bay scores 15/100 for bushfire risk. Wattamolla scores 39/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Watsons Bay or Wattamolla?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Watsons Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.27% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
